description: An umbrella term for an alleged drug trafficking network involving members of the Venezuelan Armed Forces and political officials. First used in 1993 after a scandal involving generals implicated in a CIA cocaine plot. Experts reject it as a formal organization, viewing it as journalistic shorthand for corruption. The US has designated it a Foreign Terrorist Organization, a move criticized as politically motivated.

country_links.evidence_quote: ...which sought to infiltrate Colombian gangs trafficking cocaine into the United States.
country_links.context: The Cartel of the Suns coordinated with Colombian cartels and the FARC to traffic drugs.

country_links.evidence_quote: These shipments of drugs were reportedly sent via the FARC in Colombia to the United States and Europe, with the possible assistance of Cuba.
country_links.context: The Cartel of the Suns sent drug shipments with possible assistance from Cuba.

country_links.evidence_quote: ...it is responsible for the transport of shipments though Venezuela to key hubs in Honduras, the Dominican Republic, Suriname and Europe via Africa.
country_links.context: The Cartel of the Suns transports drug shipments through the Dominican Republic.

country_links.evidence_quote: ...31 suitcases containing 1.3 tons of cocaine were discovered on an Air France flight... it was the largest seizure of cocaine recorded in mainland France.
country_links.context: The Cartel of the Suns shipped cocaine to France via Air France.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 11 November 2015, DEA agents in Haiti arrested two relatives... as they attempted to move 800 kilos of cocaine from Venezuela to the United States.
country_links.context: The Cartel of the Suns moved cocaine through Haiti.
